hyperedges.label: Phase 1 Builder Milestone Chain: M1 Backend → M1/M3 Foundation → M4 Salesman → M5 Dashboard
Data Integrity Core: Paise Money + Immutable Snapshots + RLS Enforcement + Order State Machine
Post-Milestone UI Fix Prompts: Bottombar + Dashboard UX + Column Hierarchy + Salesman Flow
Order Integrity Guarantee: RPC guards + immutable snapshots + idempotent submit
Cross-Phase Tally Integration Architecture: boundary + Phase 2 sync + catalog admin + schema
Username Auth System: design decision + provisioning runbook + RLS spec + test accounts
All T2 instrument screens share ledger aesthetic

nodes.label: S1 Login Screen — Get In Once Then Never Again
nodes.description: Phase 1 design render for the Login screen (S1). Shows two variants: S1-a default state and S1-b error states. Session persists ~30 days making this a rare-event screen. No signup, no self-service password reset — users call the office. Branded for Ganpati Enterprises (Order capture · field sales).
variants: S1-b: States — loading, wrong credentials, deactivated
nodes.norm_label: s1 login screen — get in once then never again

nodes.label: S1-a Login Default State
nodes.description: Default login screen with Ganpati Enterprises branding, email field, password field (with Show toggle), Keep me signed in checkbox (checked, ~30 days), Sign in button, and footer: 'Forgot password? Call the office to reset it.' 48px+ touch targets, email keyboard, no autocapitalize.

nodes.label: S1-b Login Error States
nodes.description: Three states shown: (1) SUBMIT LOADING — 'Signing in...' blue button with spinner; (2) ERROR WRONG CREDENTIALS — red banner 'Email or password is wrong. Check and try again.' with red-outlined password field; (3) ERROR ACCOUNT DEACTIVATED — red banner 'This account has been deactivated. Call the office to get back in.' Errors are plain sentences, never codes. Red appears ONLY for errors and Cancelled.

nodes.label: Session Persistence ~30 Days Design Decision
nodes.description: Design decision: session survives app/browser restarts, persists ~30 days. Login is a rare event — Raju (field rep persona) sees this screen roughly once a month. 'Keep me signed in' checkbox is checked by default.

nodes.label: No Self-Service Password Reset — Call the Office
nodes.description: Deliberate design constraint: no self-service password reset flow. Footer reads 'Forgot password? Call the office to reset it.' Admin/office handles account management. Deactivated accounts also require calling the office. No dead ends for the user.
nodes.norm_label: no self-service password reset — call the office

nodes.label: Red = Errors and Cancelled Only — Color System Rule
nodes.description: Global design system rule established here: Red appears ONLY for errors and Cancelled status — nowhere else in the system. Errors are plain sentences, never error codes.
nodes.norm_label: red = errors and cancelled only — color system rule

nodes.label: Ganpati Enterprises Brand Identity
nodes.description: Brand shown on login: 'GE' initials in blue square avatar, 'Ganpati Enterprises' wordmark, tagline 'Order capture · field sales'. Establishes the app is a field sales order-capture tool for a distributor named Ganpati Enterprises.

nodes.label: 48px+ Touch Target Design Constraint
nodes.description: Mobile-first design annotation: 48px+ fields and button. Email keyboard on first field. No autocapitalize. Ensures usability for field sales reps using the app on a phone.

nodes.label: S2 My Orders — Launch Pad + History Screen

properties.subtitle: Launch pad + history
properties.role: Home screen listing all orders with New Order CTA
properties.description: Four-variant design spec for the My Orders home screen. Shows order history split into TODAY and EARLIER sections, a persistent New Order thumb-zone button, offline pinned card state, empty state, and skeleton loading state.

nodes.norm_label: s2 my orders — launch pad + history screen

properties.note: Countdown ticks by the minute inside the chip; under 10m it shifts amber; never red never seconds

properties.position: Bottom thumb zone, persistent across all states
properties.label: + New Order
properties.behavior: Always tappable — stays active even during loading and empty states; history is not a prerequisite

properties.trigger: Offline order saved on device, not yet submitted
properties.card_title: Saved on phone — not submitted yet
properties.note: No order ref — refs assigned by server on submit. Auto-retry plus manual retry. Copy promises no duplicates.

properties.heading: No orders yet
properties.subtext: Take your first order — tap New Order below.

properties.pattern: Skeleton placeholder rows — NOT a spinner
properties.note: New Order stays tappable during load; history is not a prerequisite for taking an order

properties.color_shift: Shifts amber under 10m remaining; never red

nodes.label: Pick Retailer Screen (S3) — New Order Step 1
nodes.description: Mobile screen for a salesperson to pick a retailer (shop) when starting a new order. Step 1 of 3 in the new-order flow. Shows five interaction states: S3-a default recents list, S3-b live search with substring match, S3-c no-results exit into quick-add, S3-d quick-add new shop form (flagged NEW/pending-verification), S3-e resume-draft bottom sheet. User role: field salesperson.
nodes.norm_label: pick retailer screen (s3) — new order step 1

nodes.label: Retailer Search Component — Live Substring Match
nodes.description: Search bar filtering shops by name + area. Case/space-insensitive, matched substring highlighted. Recents section shown first (covers ~90% of picks). 60px tall rows showing name + area only. No result found automatically exits into quick-add with typed text carried over.
nodes.norm_label: retailer search component — live substring match

nodes.label: Quick-Add New Shop Form (S3-d)
nodes.description: Form for adding an unrecognised shop on the fly. Fields: Shop name (required, pre-filled from search), Area, Phone (numeric keyboard). Saved as NEW — pending office verification. Order can proceed immediately. CTA: 'Add & start order'. Only name is required — minimises friction while shopkeeper waits.

nodes.label: Resume Draft Order Bottom Sheet (S3-e)
nodes.description: Bottom sheet prompt shown when a local draft is found for the selected shop. Shows draft summary: item count, total (₹4,478), time saved (11:31 on this phone). CTAs: 'Continue order' or 'Start fresh'. Drafts autosave on every tap, live only on this device. Start fresh explicitly discards — never silently.

nodes.label: S4 Quick Order List — hero screen (notebook killer)

properties.screen_title: Quick Order List — the hero. The notebook killer.
properties.purpose: Full-catalog quick order entry for distributor sales; all 34 products in one grouped scroll, no pagination, no product pages, no images
variants: S4-LIVE: working demo with template variables, search, qty steppers, empty-search state
S4-b: in-cart rows with longest-name clamp (scrolled to Speakers section)
S4-c: searching 'astra' — filtered results showing 1 of 34 products, cart bar persists
S4-d: empty search state — 'No products match xyz', Clear search CTA, cart bar persists
S4-e: keypad overlay — direct quantity entry via custom 54px numeric keypad
ui_components: sticky search bar (client-side, space-insensitive, name or SKU)
category group headers (sticky while scrolling, e.g. POWER BANKS · 3, SPEAKERS · 10, CHARGING CABLES · 6)
product rows (2-line name clamp + GST-inclusive price + quantity stepper)
quantity stepper (minus / value / plus; 46×52px high-affordance blue + button)
sticky cart bar (item count + running ₹ total + Review chevron button)
keypad overlay (54px keys, 3-digit max, product name label, Cancel / Set quantity actions)
empty-search state (no dead-end: Clear search button)
design_decisions: single grouped scroll — beats 24 taps of notebook ordering
high-affordance blue + stepper filled when in-cart
sticky cart bar survives filtering — total never disappears mid-dictation
custom keypad (no OS keyboard, no focus dance)
longest product name clamps at 2 lines; price row always visible
3 items = distinct lines, so cart count reflects unique SKUs
target: 6-line dictated order in under 90 seconds, one-handed
data_shown: GST-inclusive price in ₹ (paise converted to rupees for display)

nodes.norm_label: s4 quick order list — hero screen (notebook killer)

nodes.label: Quick Order List — sticky search bar

properties.behavior: client-side, space-insensitive, searches by product name or SKU
states: no-results ('No products match xyz' + Clear search CTA)

nodes.norm_label: quick order list — sticky search bar

properties.trigger: tap quantity value on any product row

properties.note: no OS keyboard, no focus dance; Scrim-tap or Cancel discards

nodes.norm_label: quick order list — direct-entry keypad overlay

nodes.label: Quick Order List — sticky cart bar

properties.behavior: persists during search filtering and empty-search states; appears at first quantity added

nodes.norm_label: quick order list — sticky cart bar

properties.description: Last chance to adjust: lines stay editable, notes carry delivery instructions, total computed in front of shopkeeper. Failed or offline submit is loud but calm — order is safe on phone, retrying never duplicates. Confirmation only renders on confirmed server success.

properties.title: Review order
properties.subtitle: Step 3 of 3
properties.retailer_header: Sharma Electronics, Sadar Bazaar with Change link

line_items.name: Micro Usb Cable MU240 - ZB CABLE (White)

properties.stepper_grammar: Same as S4, one size down
properties.qty_controls: minus / quantity / plus stepper per line

nodes.label: S5-b: Submit Failed / Offline — Saved, Not Lost

properties.title: Review order
properties.banner_color: amber (not red — nothing is wrong with order)
properties.banner_heading: Saved on phone — not submitted yet
properties.banner_body: No network right now. Your order is safe on this phone and will also appear on Home until it goes through.
properties.cta: Retry submit (amber button with retry spinner icon)
properties.design_note: Loudest screen in the flow, still amber not red; four sync states: saved locally → submitting… → submitted ✓ → retry needed

nodes.norm_label: s5-b: submit failed / offline — saved, not lost

states.label: Submitting…
states.note: TAPS IGNORED, NO DOUBLE SUBMIT — spinner shown

properties.title: Order submitted
properties.edit_window_note: Edit or cancel from Home before then. After that, call the accountant.
properties.design_note: Ref is the headline — read out over phone in a dispute. Renders ONLY on confirmed server success; offline paths land on S5-b instead.

properties.use_case: read out over phone in a dispute

properties.description: Order remains editable or cancellable from Home until the window closes

properties.offline_handling: order safe on phone, visible on Home until submitted

nodes.label: S7 Order Detail (Salesman) — History, Disputes, Edit/Cancel
nodes.description: Order detail screen for the salesman role, showing four lifecycle states on one sheet: editable (countdown running), locked (read-only after window expiry or accountant processing), processed (terminal read-only), and cancelled (with confirm dialog). Line items are immutable snapshots — catalog price changes never rewrite history. Events shown in plain-words history log.
nodes.norm_label: s7 order detail (salesman) — history, disputes, edit/cancel

nodes.label: S7-a Editable State — Countdown Running

line_items.name: Micro Usb Cable MU240 - ZB CABLE (White)

history: Edited 12:05 — Micro Usb Cable MU240 qty 8→10
nodes.footnote: Edit reopens S4 pre-filled with these quantities. Prices shown are the submit-time snapshot.

nodes.label: S7-b Locked State — Read-Only
nodes.line_items_display: 6 lines — collapsed in this state card
nodes.banner: The edit window has ended. Call the accountant to change this order.
nodes.footnote: Locked is derived: window expiry OR the accountant processed it — the chip reads the same either way. Edit/Cancel buttons are gone, not disabled.

nodes.label: S7-c Processed State — Terminal Read-Only
history: Edited after lock today 10:55 — TYPE C TO TYPE C CABLE TT27 PLUS (BLACK) qty 5→10 · reason: shop called
nodes.banner: Booked into Tally by the office. For any change, call the accountant.
nodes.footnote: The salesman goes read-only the instant the accountant processes — even mid-countdown. After-lock edits appear here in plain words, so shop-side disputes die quickly.

nodes.label: S7-d Cancelled State + Confirm Dialog
nodes.footnote: Salesman cancel = confirm only (no reason needed while editable). Accountant cancel always logs a reason — see S9.

nodes.label: Cancel Order Confirm Dialog
nodes.title: Cancel this order?
nodes.body:
ORD-2026-1041 · Mobile Point · ₹1,255
This can't be undone.
nodes.design_note: Destructive confirm — no reason field required for salesman cancellation while order is editable

nodes.label: Edit Window Countdown Timer (Status Chip)
nodes.purpose: Shows remaining time in the salesman edit window. When window expires (or accountant processes), chip transitions to 'Submitted · locked' and action buttons disappear.

nodes.label: Plain-Words History / Event Log
nodes.source_location: HISTORY section present in all four state panels
nodes.purpose: Append-only plain-language audit trail. Records: submission, edits (with before/after quantities and reason when post-lock), window expiry, processing by accountant. Visible to salesman to enable dispute resolution without calling office.
nodes.design_principle: Plain words — no codes or jargon; after-lock edits show qty diff and reason so shop-side disputes die quickly

nodes.label: Immutable Line Items Snapshot
nodes.source_location: Line items section in S7-a panel; collapsed in S7-b
nodes.decision: Line items are a snapshot of what was ordered at submit time — catalog price changes never rewrite history. Prices shown are submit-time snapshot, not current catalog prices.
nodes.implication: Guarantees billing accuracy and immutable order record; critical for dispute resolution

properties.design_notes: Steppers are the compact 40×42 cells; figures mono. Submit-failed/offline swaps the CTA to an amber Retry over a flat amber-edged 'Saved on phone — not submitted yet' strip; retry is idempotent and the copy says so.

nodes.label: S5-off / S6 — Offline Submit / Order Confirmation

properties.screen_purpose: Two states: offline/failed submission (S5-off) and successful order submission confirmation (S6)
components: S5-off: amber strip 'Saved on phone — not submitted yet', 'RETRYING NEVER CREATES A DUPLICATE' note, Retry submit button (amber)
S6: green checkmark, ORDER SUBMITTED heading, ORD-2026-1042, Sharma Electronics ₹4,478, 'Editable until 13:42', Back to Home button (blue), View order link
properties.design_notes: S6 only renders on confirmed server success; the ref is the mono headline read out in disputes. Offline paths stay on S5 with the amber strip.

nodes.norm_label: s5-off / s6 — offline submit / order confirmation

properties.screen_purpose: Order detail view with edit window open — shows full order, history, and edit/cancel actions
components: ORD-2026-1042 header with SUBMITTED TODAY 11:42
Status chips: Submitted (blue), editable 1h 12m
Retailer: Sharma Electronics, Sadar Bazaar, phone 98765 43210
Line items with quantities and prices (same 3 items, ₹4,478 total)
NOTES: deliver Tuesday morning
HISTORY: 12:05 Edited — MU240 qty 8→10, 11:42 Submitted by you
Cancel order button (outlined)
Edit order button (blue)
properties.design_notes: Snapshot prices (submit-time), mono timestamps in the log. Edit reopens 2b pre-filled.

properties.screen_purpose: Order status state chips showing Locked, Processed, and Cancelled states with explanatory messages
components: Submitted + Locked chips: 'The edit window has ended. Call the accountant to change this order.' — No Edit/Cancel buttons (gone, not disabled)
Processed chip (green): 'Booked into Tally by the office. For any change, call the accountant.' (amber highlighted text)
Cancelled chip (red): 'Cancelled today 11:05 — by you.'
properties.design_notes: Locked is derived — window expiry or accountant processed; same chip either way. Processing flips the salesman read-only instantly.

nodes.label: S10 Pick Slip — A4 Prices Off (Godown)

properties.screen_purpose: Pick slip preview and print screen — A4 format for godown staff, prices togglable
components: ORD-2026-1042 header with pick slip label
Prices off / Prices on toggle buttons
Print button (dark)
A4 pick slip preview: GANPATI ENTERPRISES header, ORD-2026-1042, PICK SLIP label, Submitted date/time (06 Jul 2026, 11:42), Retailer: Sharma Electronics Sadar Bazaar, Ph: 98765 43210
properties.design_notes: Prices off by default for godown use — staff sees quantities without prices.

nodes.norm_label: s10 pick slip — a4 prices off (godown)

nodes.label: Print Templates: Packing Slip (S10-b) + Retailer Order Copy (S11)
nodes.description: Design render showing two printable A4 order documents side-by-side. Top: S10-b packing slip / warehouse copy (no prices) — monochrome, no fills, Qty column 30pt mono right-aligned, names verbatim/wrapping/never-truncated, empty notes box dropped. Bottom: S11 retailer order copy (same skeleton + RATE/AMOUNT/TOTAL columns, header badge flips to ORDER COPY to prevent misfiling, GST-inclusive rates, no tax lines). Both share: GANPATI ENTERPRISES header, order ORD-2026-1042, salesman Raju, retailer Sharma Electronics Sadar Bazaar, 3 line items (Micro Usb Cable x10, Adaptor x5, BT Speaker x2), delivery note, Packed-by/Checked-by signature lines, printed timestamp. S11 adds: ₹ rates, per-line amounts, TOTAL ₹4,478.
nodes.norm_label: print templates: packing slip (s10-b) + retailer order copy (s11)

nodes.label: S10-b: Packing Slip (Warehouse Copy — No Prices)
nodes.description: Warehouse/packing copy of an order. Shows QTY + ITEM columns only (no rates or amounts). Qty column: 30pt mono, right-aligned for arm's-length readability. Item names verbatim, wrapping, never truncated. Notes box present only when notes exist (empty notes drops the box). Monochrome, no fills. Footer: GANPATI ENTERPRISES · ORDER CAPTURE, printed timestamp, page N of N. Signature lines: Packed by / Checked by.
nodes.norm_label: s10-b: packing slip (warehouse copy — no prices)

nodes.label: S11: Retailer Order Copy (With Prices)
nodes.description: Retailer-facing print copy of an order. Same skeleton as S10-b but adds RATE and AMOUNT columns plus a TOTAL line. Header badge reads ORDER COPY (vs ORDER CAPTURE) to prevent warehouse/retailer misfiling. Shows: company name, order ID, submitted datetime, retailer name + address + phone, salesman name, line items with GST-inclusive rates and computed amounts, total, notes, signature lines. No separate tax lines shown.

nodes.label: Print Template Design Decisions
nodes.description: Explicit design annotations visible in render: (1) Qty column 30pt mono, right-aligned — arm's-length readability for warehouse use. (2) Item names verbatim, wrapping, never truncated. (3) Empty notes field => box is dropped entirely. (4) Monochrome, no fills. (5) Header badge flips between ORDER CAPTURE and ORDER COPY so paper copies can't be misfiled. (6) GST-inclusive rates, no tax lines. (7) Prices on for retailer copy, prices off for packing slip.

properties.purpose: Manage retailer records — verify pending shops, edit verified shops, deactivate/reactivate
Primary navigation between Orders and Retailers sections
Filter retailers table by verification status

properties.warning_text: Fix the spelling now — this exact name becomes the Tally ledger mapping in Phase 2.
properties.note: Saving spelling IS the verification act — one motion

properties.label: Foundations — instrument pass
properties.notes: Radius 2 everywhere, no shadows, ≥48px touch targets, amber = pending (never red), red = errors + Cancelled only
properties.cross_reference: Chips/steppers/figure alignment/buttons in 2c; tab bar in 3b

properties.constraint: Retailer name at verification time becomes the Tally ledger mapping in Phase 2
properties.implication: Spelling must be corrected before Save & verify — name is immutable after verification
properties.source: inline warning on pending row + annotation note

properties.description: When pending queue is empty, show flat 'All shops verified' card

properties.visual_description: Stylized receipt with zigzag perforated edges on top and bottom; two rounded horizontal lines inside representing line items or text content
properties.style: outline / stroke icon, bold weight, rounded terminals

nodes.label: Apple Touch Icon
nodes.description: Receipt/invoice icon used as Apple touch icon. Outline-style line art of a receipt with zigzag torn edges at top and bottom, two rounded pill-shaped horizontal lines inside representing line items. Dark navy (#0d0d1a) on white background. Minimal, clean icon style.

nodes.label: Browser Favicon
nodes.description: Receipt/invoice icon used as browser favicon. Visually identical to apple-icon.png: outline-style line art of a receipt with zigzag torn edges at top and bottom, two rounded pill-shaped horizontal lines inside. Dark navy (#0d0d1a) on white background.

nodes.label: App Brand Identity
nodes.description: The app uses a receipt/invoice icon as its brand mark across all icon sizes. This anchors the product identity around order tracking, sales receipts, and transaction management — consistent with the direct-sales project domain. Monochrome palette (near-black on white) signals a clean, professional, minimal aesthetic.

links.source_location: v0 plan evolved into the current PLAN.md
§Phase 1 Path B: Quick Order List (Chosen Standard)
Same planning era — solution and plan authored together

AI proposed solution to owner's described requirements
Both authored in same planning session; problem statement motivates the proposed solution
§1 Our statuses only: Submitted / Processed / Cancelled
§1 Money is integer paise; render with formatRupees
Fixes the S8 orders table originally built in M5
Revamp of S8 Orders page built in M5
§2 Source of truth: design/phase1-design-spec.md S8/S9/S10/S11 screens
§preamble — salesman order flow (M4) done; this milestone is M5
§README Stack: Money is always integer paise
§2 getUser() not getSession() for gating; RLS is the wall
§0 Backend and app foundation are done — M4 builds on M1+M3
§0 The entire Postgres backend is done and reviewer-verified — builds on M1
§Your single deliverable is one file: Prompts/phase1-design-prompt.md
Both are post-M5 UI fix prompts targeting the accountant dashboard
Fixes the S3/S4/S5 screens built in M4
§3 Nine design principles — dense ledger, text-first, mono figures
§2 Source of truth: design/phase1-design-spec.md (authored from phase1-design-prompt) is the conformance target
§What this is — hairline rules, 2px corner radius, Space Grotesk/JetBrains Mono
§1 Idempotency via a client-generated order UUID
§7 Do NOT — use floats for money
Idempotency ensures exactly-once submit semantics for the state machine
Snapshots are taken at the submitted state transition; tied to the state machine
RLS + guard_order_transition enforce valid state transitions
mobile chrome/statusbar visible in S1-a, annotation 48px+
footer text: Forgot password? Call the office to reset it.
Keep me signed in checkbox — checked by default, ~30 days
deactivated error: Call the office to get back in

hyperedges.label: Phase 1 Builder Milestone Chain: M1 Backend → M1/M3 Foundation → M4 Salesman → M5 Dashboard
Data Integrity Core: Paise Money + Immutable Snapshots + RLS Enforcement + Order State Machine
Post-Milestone UI Fix Prompts: Bottombar + Dashboard UX + Column Hierarchy + Salesman Flow
Order Integrity Guarantee: RPC guards + immutable snapshots + idempotent submit
Cross-Phase Tally Integration Architecture: boundary + Phase 2 sync + catalog admin + schema
Username Auth System: design decision + provisioning runbook + RLS spec + test accounts
All T2 instrument screens share ledger aesthetic
